repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
## Summary

This PR extends the `auto_cpdef` feature to automatically promote eligible methods in `@cclass`-decorated classes to `cpdef` functions. 
This enables big performance wins with C-level optimization of large codebases with minimal changes, while still allowing selective exclusion of methods that are incompatible or error-prone.

### How it works

- **Automatic method promotion**: Methods in classes with `auto_cpdef=True` directive are automatically promoted to `cpdef` when compatible, including final classes where optimization is most effective
- **Fine-grained control**: New directives `no_ccall` allow excluding specific methods from promotion

This brings the same optimization benefits previously available only for module-level functions to instance methods within `@cclass`-decorated classes.

### Test Cases:

New test file `tests/run/auto_cpdef_classes.py` validates:
- Method promotion in base classes

comment_data.body:
I also think this error message is something that *can't* be ignored for `public` functions. If it has optional arguments then it doesn't have a simple C signature that can be put in a header file.

Which should be fine because `auto_cpdef` shouldn't require public.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -2791,7 +2793,7 @@ def analyse_declarations(self, env):
         self.args = declarator.args
 
         opt_arg_count = self.cfunc_declarator.optional_arg_count
-        if (self.visibility == 'public' or self.api) and opt_arg_count:
+        if (self.visibility == 'public' or self.api) and opt_arg_count and not self.promoted:
             error(self.cfunc_declarator.pos,
                   "Function with optional arguments may not be declared public or api")

comment_data.body:
This is the fundamental, massive problem with the proposed change.

If I change this to just `import a` I get:

```
undefined symbol: __pyx_f_1b_1B_m
```

`public` (for classes and functions) is not how we shared things between Cython modules. `public` is a mechanism for people writing their own C code who want to be able to call functions defined in Cython or to use classes defined in Cython.  It puts everything onto the dll-loader/operating system, and so it requires the symbols used by `a` to be available at the point you import `a`.

The mechanism for sharing things between Cython modules is to use the `cimport` mechanism. In this case Cython controls the loading of symbols so can avoid strict restrictions on the import order.

In pyx mode, `cdef public class` declarations look like

https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/fe937cc07680cdd75714b04be3a1ad965ce3c03f/tests/compile/a_capi.pyx#L11-L12

Specifying the cname of the object and the type is not optional. This is because they are designed for people writing their own C code who will want to use the generated header files and who will want to know what the name is.

It is not acceptable that `public` works differently in pyx mode than in does in py mode.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+# cython: auto_cpdef=True
+# mode:run
+# tag: directive,auto_cpdef
+from cython import cclass, int, final, public, no_ccall
+
+
+@cclass
+class Base:
+    """Simplest base case"""
+
+    def ret3(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        return 3
+
+@final
+@cclass
+class BaseFinal:
+    """Final Class"""
+
+    def ret2(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        # being final, it can be optimized
+        return 2
+
+
+# Auto cpdef causes the fn() function becomes public, thus Vector needs to be public too.
+@public
+@cclass
+class Vector:
+    a: int
+    b: int
+
+    def __init__(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
+        self.a = a
+        self.b = b
+
+    def __add__(self, other: 'Vector') -> 'Vector':
+        return Vector(self.a + other.a, self.b + other.b)
+
+    def length_sq(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        return self.a*self.a + self.b*self.b
+
+    def __eq__(self, other: 'Vector') -> bool:
+        return self.a == other.a and self.b == other.b
+
+    def __repr__(self) -> str:
+        return f"Vector({self.a}, {self.b})"
+
+
+def fn() -> Vector:
+    a: int = BaseFinal().ret2()  # optimized direct C-call of promoted to cpdef
+    b: int = Base().ret3()  # optimized vtable-call of promoted cpdef
+    return Vector(a, b)
+
+
+def test_vector(v):
+    """
+    Just test a simple class with auto_cpdef enabled.
+
+    >>> Base().ret3()
+    3
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) + Vector(4, 5))
+    Vector(6, 8)
+    >>> test_vector(fn().length_sq())
+    13
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) == Vector(4, 5))  # special method semantics preserved
+    False
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) == Vector(2, 3))
+    True
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3).__eq__(Vector(4, 5)))  # special method semantics preserved
+    False
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3).__eq__(Vector(2, 3)))
+    True
+    """
+    return v
+
+
+@public

comment_data.body: @da-woods  Thanks for taking the time of reviewing this change, I will remove the public directive, I added it because I suspected it was required to make auto_cpdef work for class methods since the simpler surgical change was suffering of methods not being public when they got optimized. Then, I realize it is not required due the imported method pointers approach at module level.

right, as I said I realize it is not required but didnt change the test.
I will remove this directive.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+# cython: auto_cpdef=True
+# mode:run
+# tag: directive,auto_cpdef
+from cython import cclass, int, final, public, no_ccall
+
+
+@cclass
+class Base:
+    """Simplest base case"""
+
+    def ret3(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        return 3
+
+@final
+@cclass
+class BaseFinal:
+    """Final Class"""
+
+    def ret2(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        # being final, it can be optimized
+        return 2
+
+
+# Auto cpdef causes the fn() function becomes public, thus Vector needs to be public too.
+@public
+@cclass
+class Vector:
+    a: int
+    b: int
+
+    def __init__(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
+        self.a = a
+        self.b = b
+
+    def __add__(self, other: 'Vector') -> 'Vector':
+        return Vector(self.a + other.a, self.b + other.b)
+
+    def length_sq(self) -> int:
+        # This function is automatically promoted to cpdef
+        return self.a*self.a + self.b*self.b
+
+    def __eq__(self, other: 'Vector') -> bool:
+        return self.a == other.a and self.b == other.b
+
+    def __repr__(self) -> str:
+        return f"Vector({self.a}, {self.b})"
+
+
+def fn() -> Vector:
+    a: int = BaseFinal().ret2()  # optimized direct C-call of promoted to cpdef
+    b: int = Base().ret3()  # optimized vtable-call of promoted cpdef
+    return Vector(a, b)
+
+
+def test_vector(v):
+    """
+    Just test a simple class with auto_cpdef enabled.
+
+    >>> Base().ret3()
+    3
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) + Vector(4, 5))
+    Vector(6, 8)
+    >>> test_vector(fn().length_sq())
+    13
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) == Vector(4, 5))  # special method semantics preserved
+    False
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3) == Vector(2, 3))
+    True
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3).__eq__(Vector(4, 5)))  # special method semantics preserved
+    False
+    >>> test_vector(Vector(2, 3).__eq__(Vector(2, 3)))
+    True
+    """
+    return v
+
+
+@public

comment_data.body:
I'm a little wary of this change. It seems like it at least has the potential to change how compiler directives and pxd files work together.

(i.e. I'd rather have the least invasive version of this overall change)

comment_data.body: Back in 2022 I was apparently in favour of removing generate pxi completely: https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/5047. I think I still agree with my past self here. It certainly doesn't seem part of auto_cpdef

commit.message: Back to use private as default
Fix optimizations for direct method calls
Fix optimizations for inline methods in pxd
Remove public directive, visibility related changes, super method optimization
Fix the one case preventing many cases

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

title: Auto cpdef extension to class methods
